title: Committed review flags shortened names for new cross-resource ID fields

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark that committed review keeps new public cross-resource ID fields aligned with provider naming rules instead of allowing shortened day-one names to ship.
provenance.genericCondition: Synthetic fixture models a new public resource surface that introduces a shortened cross-resource ID field name across schema, list resource, and docs.

description: A committed-review scenario where a brand-new resource and its companion docs expose a new cross-resource ID field as `scheduler_id` even though provider naming rules require the full referenced resource name without the `azurerm_` prefix, such as `durable_task_scheduler_id`. The correct review should flag the shortened day-one public field name as a naming issue.

scope.notes: Synthetic fixture models a new public resource surface that introduces a shortened cross-resource ID field name across schema, list resource, and docs.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should flag the new public cross-resource ID field name because it shortens the referenced resource name instead of using the full `{resource_name}_id` pattern.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not accept a shortened new cross-resource ID field name such as `scheduler_id` when the referenced resource name is longer and no evidence-backed naming exception is present.

notes: Use this case to benchmark that committed review keeps new public cross-resource ID fields aligned with provider naming rules instead of allowing shortened day-one names to ship.
